'''Reubenn Rennie''' (born 22 October 1995) is a [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Rennie was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]]. He is of [[Cook Islands]] descent.<ref>[https://www.zerotackle.com/players/reubenn-rennie/ Zero Tackle profile]</ref> His brother [[Vincent Rennie]] is a fellow [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands international]].<ref>[https://www.goal-click.com/football-photography-stories/vincent-rennie Vincent Rennie, Cook Islands]</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Rennie played for the [[Melbourne Storm]]-[[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] joint team in 2014.<ref>[https://leagueunlimited.com/people/show/reubenn-rennie leagueunlimited stats]</ref>

Rennie played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in 2015 and 2016.<ref>[https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2015/12/15/rookie-on-the-rise-reubenn-rennie/ Rookie on the Rise: Reubenn Rennie]</ref>

Rennie played for the Newtown club in 2017 and 2018.<ref>[https://www.newtownjets.com/category/2017-team-lists/ "Newtown Team List v North Sydney 26th August, 2017"]</ref>

Rennie played for the [[Mount Pritchard Mounties]] between 2019 and 2021.<ref>[https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2017/04/08/mounties-hold-out-fast-finishing-tigers/ Mounties Hold Out Fast-Finishing Tigers]</ref>

Rennie re-joined the Newtown side ahead of the [[2022 NSW Cup]] season.<ref>[https://nothingbutleague.com/player/reubenn-rennie/ Nothingbutleague stats]</ref>

===International career===
Rennie made his senior international debut for the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] June 2016 against [[Lebanon national rugby league team|Lebanon]]. In 2022 he was named in the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup squads#Cook Islands|Cook Islands squad]] for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-squad-lists/ Full list of every squad at the Rugby League World Cup 2021]</ref>

In the second group stage match against {{rlnt|Papua New Guinea}}, Rennie was sent to the [[Penalty box|sin bin]] for a dangerous lifting tackle during the Cook Islands 32–16 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/world-cup-2022-sin-bins-papua-new-guinea-v-cook-islands-watch-video-mackenzie-yei-rua-ngatikaura-wellington-albert-reubenn-rennie-james-graham/news-story/59996f6766b73f57e324208b5e3b689a/|title='You cannot do that': Brutal shot from 115kg PNG enforcer preludes Cook Islander's brain explosion|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=26 October 2022 }}</ref>
